currentWave: Four Netlify Function HTTP endpoints now live-verified: tickets.js (F019/F023), organizations.js (F001), work-queue.js (F051), approvals.js (F016). The session-cookie-to-RBAC auth bridge (care_hub_auth.js) now handles all three real authorization shapes: org-membership-backed (customer roles), platform-wide (platform_admin from legacy 'admin'), and per-resource (technician from legacy 'staff'). 17 more endpoints remain; a UI still doesn't exist.
workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main. Dylan granted bypass permission (2026-07-14) to take care of everything without interruption, with that one hard constraint. Netlify (via Chrome) remains available for local setup/testing only, never for deploy or touching production.

migrationStatus: EXECUTED against the live Neon database (Session 13), 37 tables. No schema changes this session -- all four endpoints reuse existing tables.
deploymentStatus: not-deployed. Four Netlify Function endpoints now exist and are locally verified; nothing is deployed or reachable from the public internet.
implementedFunctions: 41 functions have real, tested domain/policy/persistence logic; 28 with real Postgres persistence. 6 are additionally VERIFIED against a live database.

This session (16): three more Netlify Function HTTP endpoints -- organizations.js (F001), work-queue.js (F051), approvals.js (F016) -- plus two new rbac.js capabilities (workqueue.view for platform_admin, approval.view for org_owner) and two legacy-role bridges (admin->platform_admin, staff->technician) in care_hub_auth.js, replacing an inconsistency from Session 15 where technicians needed an unnecessary organization_memberships row.

F035/F036/F038/F039/F040 (check-EXECUTION engines not built -- needs the website-audit spec adapted)

Customer account registration model: open vs invite-only (audit F033) -- still the most urgent remaining decision: without it there is no automatic path from a new Blobs customer account to a Care Hub organization_memberships row, so every customer-facing endpoint needs a membership manually created to be reachable at all. filesRequiringReview: docs/development/OWNER_DECISIONS.md (items #1, #2, #3 resolved; F002/registration model is now the sole most-urgent remaining decision)
docs/development/REQUIREMENTS_TRACEABILITY.md (Post-Session-15 section documents the 3 new endpoints and the two rbac.js additions)

DECISION_LOG.md Session 16 entry explains why platform_admin currently cannot use staff-only endpoints (no ticket.view/ticket.work capability) -- worth Dylan's attention for a single-person operation where the one admin account also does technician work
knownRisks: Only 6 of 28 persistence-layer functions have been live-smoke-tested at the store layer, though 4 now have HTTP-layer live verification too (organizations, tickets, approvals, work-queue) -- everything else is schema-verified but not individually exercised live.

netlify dev's local Blobs emulation still could not be reached as of Session 15 -- not attempted again this session since the DI seam makes it unnecessary for testing; still worth Dylan's attention if full local netlify dev testing (e.g. for the legacy auth endpoints themselves) is wanted later.
Netlify Blobs' users/sessions/tokens stores (F003/F004) remain unmigrated by design
Existing Blobs records (documents, messages, notifications, favorites, leads) still need organization_id backfilled once real organizations exist -- tracked in MIGRATION_PLAN.md, not yet scheduled
No migration-runner tool is set up yet -- one-off root-level scripts, deleted after use, remain the pattern

The live database now contains smoke-test data across several sessions (multiple orgs, memberships, tickets, one assignment, one approved approval) plus 5 real entitlement_limits rows -- a mix of test and real data, not an empty or purely synthetic database

usage_records has no DB-level uniqueness constraint on (organization_id, plan_key, usage_key, period_start) -- fetch-then-write pattern used instead, more exposed to a lost-update race under real concurrency than most stores
F057's verifier/logger is ready but nothing calls it yet -- no payment webhook infrastructure exists at all
F051's cross-org query has no pagination -- fine at zero real data, will need LIMIT/cursor handling before real staff-scale data exists (SYS-API-005)

platform_admin cannot use staff-only endpoints (work-queue.js, and any future ticket.view/ticket.work-gated endpoint) under the current rbac.js capability map -- a faithful reflection of Session 1's design (technicians run day-to-day tickets, platform_admin manages the platform), not a bug, but worth revisiting for a single-person operation where Dylan is both roles

Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs; new care_hub_documents/file_assets Postgres tables use an opaque storageRef instead, but the actual object-storage provider is still undecided
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) -- still not extracted
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention
src/settings/blobsSettingsStore.js remains Blobs-backed (F056 stayed on Blobs by design)
CSV export (src/export/csvExport.js, F053) has no formula-injection guard for spreadsheet software -- open finding from Session 9's security review

17 more Netlify Function HTTP endpoints remain to be built to expose the rest of the persistence layer, following the now-established pattern (care_hub_auth.js + deps-injection seam + honest rbac.js action if none exists yet)
releaseRecommendation: NOT READY. All three resolvable owner-decision blockers (data store, pricing/plan-limits) are done, and four HTTP endpoints now prove the whole stack works end to end -- auth, RBAC (including platform-wide and per-resource authorization shapes), policy engines, Postgres -- all live-verified. 41 functions have real tested logic, 28 with real Postgres persistence, 438 passing unit tests. Remaining work: 17 more HTTP endpoints, a UI, and 7 open owner decisions -- registration model (F002) is the clear next-most-urgent, since every customer-facing endpoint requires a manually-created organization membership until it's resolved.
